test_description: This test case evaluates the system's ability to retrieve and synthesize information from multiple, separate document sections to answer a complex question.
question: [Fill] In the novel 90-nm logic technology featuring strained silicon, uniaxial tensile stress is introduced into the nMOSFET channel primarily by the stress and thickness of the _____ blank capping layer, while compressive stress in the pMOSFET channel is introduced from selective epitaxial chemical vapor deposition of _____ blank in the source and drain regions.

gold_context_sources.evidence: An Si recess etch is inserted post spacer formation and followed by selective epitaxial chemical vapor deposition (CVD) of Si Ge. The process flow steps are shown in Fig. 3. In this paper 17% germanium concentration is used which has a lattice spacing larger than Si. The mismatch in the Si Ge to Si lattice causes the smaller lattice constant Si channel to be under compressive stress. Longitudinal uniaxial tensile and out-of-plane compressive stress is introduced into the nMOSFET by engineering the stress and thickness of the Si nitride-capping layer.

The unique advantage of this uniaxial stress Si process flow is that on the same wafer compressive stress is introduced into the p-type and tensile stress in the nMOSFETs to improve both the electron and hole mobility. Since the nitride capping layer is already present to support unlanded contacts, only a few new process steps are introduced at less than a 2% wafer cost increase. Furthermore, by confining the Si Ge to the source and drain and introducing it late in the process flow, integration challenges are reduced.

ground_truth_answer: The correct answer is [Si nitride, SiGe]. Reasoning: The strained silicon process flow described in the document explains that the uniaxial tensile stress in the nMOSFET channel is introduced by engineering the stress and thickness of the silicon nitride (Si nitride) capping layer. Meanwhile, compressive stress in the pMOSFET channel is introduced from selective epitaxial chemical vapor deposition (CVD) of silicon germanium (SiGe) in the source and drain regions. This combination allows for independent stress engineering of n- and p-type MOSFETs on the same wafer, enhancing mobility and device performance.
